task_groups.title: Write Tests — OAuth Provider Registry and Redirect URI Allowlist

subtasks.title: Write tests for GET /api/v1/auth/providers endpoint
details: Create test file internal/auth/provider_registry_test.go
Write unit test: provider registry stores GitHub entry with name, authorize_url, scopes='user:email'; token_url and client_secret not in response (TS-02-1)
Write unit test: configured URL overrides replace defaults in the provider response (TS-02-2)
Write unit test: adding a second provider (e.g. 'gitlab') appears in GET /api/v1/auth/providers without modifying auth flow code (TS-02-3)
Write integration test: GET /api/v1/auth/providers returns HTTP 200 with correct shape; no secrets exposed (TS-02-4)
Write edge case test: empty provider registry returns HTTP 200 with providers:[] (TS-02-E1)
All tests use go test table-driven style; mock GitHub OAuth endpoints using httptest

subtasks.title: Write tests for redirect URI allowlist validation
details: Create test file internal/auth/allowlist_test.go
Write integration test: dev mode (no external_url) allows http://localhost:8080 and http://localhost:3000; rejects http://otherhost:3000 (TS-02-10)
Write integration test: production mode (external_url='https://app.example.com') allows exact match only; rejects different port (TS-02-11)
Write unit test: path component is not considered; only scheme+host+port compared (TS-02-12)
Write edge case test: localhost.evil.com and mylocalhost:3000 rejected in dev mode (TS-02-E13)
Test the allowlist checker function directly as a unit test in addition to HTTP-level tests

subtasks.title: Write tests for POST /api/v1/auth/callback — happy path and transaction atomicity
details: Create test file internal/auth/callback_test.go
Write integration test: full callback flow returns HTTP 200 with user object and api_key; DB committed in single transaction (TS-02-5)
Write integration test: new user created with status='active' and UUID v4 id when no existing (provider, provider_id) record (TS-02-6)
Write integration test: existing user updated (username, email) from provider; provider_id unchanged; updated_at bumped only if values changed (TS-02-7)
Write integration test: key_id is 8 base62 chars, secret is 32 base62 chars from crypto/rand; SHA-256 hash stored; expires_at computed correctly; token format af_<key_id>_<secret> (TS-02-8)
Write integration test: previous active key is revoked; new key created; both operations in same transaction; no-op if no prior active key (TS-02-9)
Use httptest.NewServer to mock GitHub token_url and userinfo_url; use in-memory SQLite for DB

subtasks.title: Write tests for POST /api/v1/auth/callback — error paths
details: Write unit test: unrecognized provider returns HTTP 400; no exchange call made (TS-02-E2)
Write unit test: invalid expires value (e.g. 45, -1, 'ninety') returns HTTP 400 listing accepted values; no exchange call made (TS-02-E3)
Write unit test: non-allowlisted redirect_uri returns HTTP 400; no exchange call made (TS-02-E4)
Write integration test: provider code exchange HTTP 500 returns HTTP 502; no DB writes (TS-02-E5)
Write integration test: provider returns null/empty email returns HTTP 400; no user or key created (TS-02-E6)
Write integration test: invalid GitHub login field (spaces, special chars) returns HTTP 400 (TS-02-E7)
Write integration test: case-insensitive username conflict with different (provider, provider_id) returns HTTP 409 (TS-02-E8)
Write integration test: blocked user returns HTTP 403; no key created; status unchanged (TS-02-E9)
Write integration test: missing external_url in production mode returns HTTP 500 (TS-02-E10)
Write integration test: DB transaction failure rolls back all operations; returns HTTP 500 (TS-02-E11)
Write integration test: provider timeout returns HTTP 502; bounded by context deadline (TS-02-E12)

subtasks.title: Write tests for API key generation security properties
details: Create test file internal/keys/generate_test.go
Write unit test: key generation uses crypto/rand; no math/rand import in key generation package; generate 1000 key_ids and assert no collisions (TS-02-34)
Write integration test: after callback, api_keys row has secret_hash = SHA-256(returned_secret); no plaintext secret in any DB column (TS-02-35)
Write unit test: generate 100 keys; every key_id matches ^[0-9A-Za-z]{8}$; every secret matches ^[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$; every token matches ^af_[0-9A-Za-z]{8}_[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$ (TS-02-36)

checks: go test ./internal/auth/... ./internal/keys/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestProvider|TestAllowlist|TestCallback|TestKeyGen' — all new test files compile and tests are correctly skipped (not yet passing) due to missing implementation
go vet ./internal/auth/... ./internal/keys/... — no vet warnings in test files
Test files reference correct requirement IDs: 02-REQ-1.x, 02-REQ-2.x, 02-REQ-3.x, 02-REQ-11.x
All test_spec_refs TS-02-1 through TS-02-9, TS-02-10 through TS-02-12, TS-02-34 through TS-02-36, TS-02-E1 through TS-02-E12, TS-02-E13 have corresponding test functions

subtasks.title: Write tests for POST /api/v1/users (admin create user)
details: Create test file internal/users/create_test.go
Write integration test: admin creates user; HTTP 201; UUID v4 id; status='active'; no api_keys row; provider_id excluded from response (TS-02-13)
Write unit test: unregistered provider string accepted; warning logged; user created (TS-02-14)
Write unit test: invalid username (bad_user!, 40-char) and empty provider_id each return HTTP 400 (TS-02-15)
Write integration test: case-insensitive username collision returns HTTP 409; duplicate (provider, provider_id) returns HTTP 409 (TS-02-16)
Write edge case: invalid username/empty provider_id — no DB row inserted (TS-02-E14)
Write edge case: username 'bob' conflicts with existing 'Bob' case-insensitively; same (provider, provider_id) conflicts (TS-02-E15)
Write edge case: non-admin user calling POST /api/v1/users returns HTTP 403 (TS-02-E16)

subtasks.title: Write tests for GET /api/v1/users and GET /api/v1/users/:id
details: Create test file internal/users/list_get_test.go
Write integration test: GET /api/v1/users returns HTTP 200 with all users ordered by created_at ASC; provider_id omitted from each entry (TS-02-17)
Write integration test: GET /api/v1/users returns HTTP 200 with empty users:[] when no records (TS-02-18)
Write integration test: GET /api/v1/users/:id returns full user object including provider_id and team_memberships array with team_id, team_name, role fields (TS-02-19)
Write edge case: GET /api/v1/users with non-admin API key returns HTTP 403 (TS-02-E17)
Write edge case: GET /api/v1/users/:id for non-existent UUID returns HTTP 404 with structured error body (TS-02-E18)
Write edge case: GET /api/v1/users/:id with non-admin API key returns HTTP 403 (TS-02-E19)

subtasks.title: Write tests for PUT /api/v1/users/:id (mixed-auth update)
details: Create test file internal/users/update_test.go
Write integration test: user updates own full_name; null/empty clears field; updated_at bumped only when value changes; provider_id included; team_memberships excluded from response (TS-02-20)
Write integration test: admin updates status on any user; updated_at bumped; provider_id included; team_memberships excluded (TS-02-21)
Write integration test: no-op PUT (identical values or empty body) returns HTTP 200 with unmodified object; updated_at not bumped (TS-02-22)
Write integration test: admin updating their own record operates with admin-level permissions; can set status on themselves (TS-02-23)
Write edge case: non-admin updating another user's record returns HTTP 403; no update (TS-02-E20)
Write edge case: non-admin including status field returns HTTP 403 regardless of other fields (TS-02-E21)
Write edge case: PUT for non-existent user ID returns HTTP 404 (TS-02-E22)

subtasks.title: Write tests for username validation and updated_at semantics
details: Create test file internal/users/validation_test.go
Write unit test: usernames 'alice-bob' accepted; 'alice_bob', 'alice bob', 40-char string rejected with HTTP 400 (TS-02-37)
Write integration test: 'Alice' created with HTTP 201 and username preserved as 'Alice'; subsequent 'alice' conflicts with HTTP 409 (TS-02-38)
Write integration test: identical full_name in PUT does not bump updated_at; empty body also does not bump (TS-02-39)
Write edge case: usernames with underscore, space, special chars return HTTP 400; no user record created (TS-02-E28)
Write edge case: 40-char username returns HTTP 400; no user record created (TS-02-E29)

checks: go test ./internal/users/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestCreate|TestList|TestGet|TestUpdate|TestValidation' — all new test files compile
go vet ./internal/users/... — no vet warnings in test files
Test files reference correct requirement IDs: 02-REQ-4.x, 02-REQ-5.x, 02-REQ-6.x, 02-REQ-7.x, 02-REQ-12.x, 02-REQ-13.x
All test_spec_refs TS-02-13 through TS-02-23, TS-02-37 through TS-02-39, TS-02-E14 through TS-02-E22, TS-02-E28, TS-02-E29 have corresponding test functions

task_groups.title: Write Tests — API Key Management Endpoints and Property Tests

subtasks.title: Write tests for GET /api/v1/keys list endpoint
details: Create test file internal/keys/list_test.go
Write integration test: admin token returns all keys across all users ordered by created_at ASC; no secret or token field in entries (TS-02-24)
Write integration test: user API key returns only that user's keys including expired and revoked historical ones; ordered by created_at ASC (TS-02-25)
Write unit test: no entry in keys array contains 'status', 'is_active', or 'is_valid' fields (TS-02-26)
Write edge case: workspace token returns HTTP 403 (TS-02-E23)

subtasks.title: Write tests for POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh endpoint
details: Create test file internal/keys/refresh_test.go
Write integration test: refresh generates new 32-char base62 secret; stores SHA-256 hash; recalculates expires_at using original N-day duration; returns plaintext secret and token (TS-02-27)
Write integration test: expired (non-revoked) key can be refreshed; expires_at recalculated from now (TS-02-28)
Write integration test: admin can refresh key of blocked user; HTTP 200 (TS-02-29)
Write unit test: refresh ignores caller-supplied expires body field; always reuses original duration (TS-02-30)
Write edge case: non-existent key_id returns HTTP 404; revoked key treated as non-existent returns HTTP 404 (TS-02-E24)
Write edge case: non-admin refreshing another user's key returns HTTP 403; secret_hash unchanged (TS-02-E25)

subtasks.title: Write tests for DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id revoke endpoint
details: Create test file internal/keys/revoke_test.go
Write integration test: DELETE sets revoked_at on first revocation; returns HTTP 204 with no body (TS-02-31)
Write integration test: DELETE on already-revoked key returns HTTP 204; revoked_at timestamp unchanged (idempotent) (TS-02-32)
Write integration test: admin can revoke key of blocked user; HTTP 204; revoked_at set (TS-02-33)
Write edge case: DELETE for non-existent key_id returns HTTP 404 (TS-02-E26)
Write edge case: non-admin revoking another user's key returns HTTP 403; revoked_at not set (TS-02-E27)

subtasks.title: Write property and smoke tests
details: Create test file internal/keys/properties_test.go for key invariant checks
Write property test TS-02-P1: after any sequence of logins, at most one api_keys row per user has revoked_at IS NULL AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now())
Write property test TS-02-P2: for every key created or refreshed, secret_hash == sha256(plaintext_secret); no column contains plaintext
Write property test TS-02-P3: after revocation, no refresh or re-auth can clear revoked_at
Write property test TS-02-P4: no two users table rows have the same LOWER(username)
Write property test TS-02-P5: inject failure at each transaction step; assert DB unchanged (atomicity)
Write property test TS-02-P6: instrument key generation to count crypto/rand.Read calls; assert >= 1 and <= 5 per generation; no math/rand usage
Write property test TS-02-P7: no-op PUT does not bump updated_at for any user
Write property test TS-02-P8: provider_id column value never changes after initial creation across multiple callbacks
Create test file internal/integration/smoke_test.go for smoke tests TS-02-SMOKE-1 through TS-02-SMOKE-4

checks: go test ./internal/keys/... ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestList|TestRefresh|TestRevoke|TestProperty|TestSmoke' — all new test files compile
go vet ./internal/keys/... ./internal/integration/... — no vet warnings
All test_spec_refs TS-02-24 through TS-02-33, TS-02-P1 through TS-02-P8, TS-02-SMOKE-1 through TS-02-SMOKE-4, TS-02-E23 through TS-02-E27 have corresponding test functions
Smoke test file imports only real (non-stub) components with GitHub endpoints mocked via httptest

subtasks.title: Define the Provider interface and registry data structures
details: Create internal/auth/provider.go

Define Provider interface with methods: AuthorizeURL() string, ExchangeCode(ctx context.Context, code, redirectURI string) (*TokenResponse, error), GetUserInfo(ctx context.Context, token string) (*UserInfo, error)
Define ProviderConfig struct: Name string, AuthorizeURL string, TokenURL string, UserInfoURL string, Scopes string, ClientID string, ClientSecret string
Define Registry struct holding map[string]Provider; NewRegistry() *Registry; Register(name string, p Provider); Lookup(name string) (Provider, bool)
Define UserInfo struct: ID string (provider_id), Login string (username), Email string, Name string
All fields use Go idiomatic naming; no panic('not implemented') stubs left in exported methods

subtasks.title: Implement GitHub provider
details: Create internal/auth/github_provider.go
Implement GitHubProvider struct satisfying Provider interface
AuthorizeURL() returns configured authorize_url (default: 'https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize')
ExchangeCode() POSTs to token_url with client_id, client_secret, code, redirect_uri; parses JSON response for access_token; returns HTTP 502 on failure or non-2xx
GetUserInfo() GETs userinfo_url with Bearer token; parses JSON for login (username), email, id (provider_id)
Apply context deadline to all outbound HTTP calls; cancel via context on timeout
NewGitHubProvider(cfg ProviderConfig) *GitHubProvider constructor; URL fields default to GitHub well-known URLs if not overridden
Default scopes: 'user:email'

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/auth/providers handler
details: Create internal/auth/handlers.go
Implement GetProvidersHandler(registry *Registry) echo.HandlerFunc
Build response: iterate over registry entries; for each: return {name, authorize_url (base URL, no state param), scopes}
Never include client_secret, token_url, or userinfo_url in response
Return HTTP 200 with {providers: [...]}; return empty array if no providers registered
Register route: GET /api/v1/auth/providers — no auth middleware required (public endpoint)

subtasks.title: Implement redirect URI allowlist validator
details: Create internal/auth/allowlist.go
Implement Allowlist struct with IsAllowed(redirectURI string) bool method
Extract origin (scheme+host+port) from redirectURI using net/url.Parse(); path component is ignored
Dev mode (external_url absent): allow any origin where host == 'localhost' (exact match, not substring); reject localhost.evil.com, mylocalhost, etc.
Production mode (external_url present): allow only if origin exactly matches external_url scheme+host+port
NewAllowlist(externalURL string, devMode bool) (*Allowlist, error) constructor
Return HTTP 500 if production mode detected but external_url is unset (config error — do not fall back to permissive list)

checks: go test ./internal/auth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestProvider|TestGitHub|TestGetProviders|TestAllowlist' — all TS-02-1 through TS-02-4 and TS-02-10 through TS-02-12, TS-02-E1, TS-02-E13 tests pass
go vet ./internal/auth/... — no vet warnings
02-REQ-1.1, 02-REQ-1.2, 02-REQ-1.3, 02-REQ-1.4 acceptance criteria met: registry stores GitHub entry; URL overrides work; public endpoint returns correct shape
02-REQ-3.1, 02-REQ-3.2, 02-REQ-3.3 acceptance criteria met: localhost dev mode permissive; production strict; path excluded from comparison
No panic('not implemented') or bare return nil stubs remain in internal/auth/

task_groups.title: Implement API Key Generation and Crypto Utilities

subtasks.title: Implement base62 encoder and crypto/rand key material generator
details: Create internal/keys/generate.go
Implement base62Encode(b []byte) string using charset [0-9A-Za-z]
Implement GenerateKeyID() (string, error): read ceil(8*6/6)=8 bytes from crypto/rand; base62-encode to exactly 8 chars
Implement GenerateSecret() (string, error): read ceil(32*6/6)=32 bytes from crypto/rand; base62-encode to exactly 32 chars
Import ONLY 'crypto/rand' — do not import 'math/rand' anywhere in this file
Return (string, error) tuples following Go conventions; propagate crypto/rand errors
BuildToken(keyID, secret string) string: returns 'af_' + keyID + '_' + secret

subtasks.title: Implement SHA-256 secret hashing and key expiry computation
details: Create internal/keys/hash.go
Implement HashSecret(secret string) string: SHA-256 of secret; return lowercase hex string
Implement ComputeExpiresAt(expires int, from time.Time) *time.Time: returns nil when expires==0; otherwise returns pointer to from + (24h * expires days)
Ensure the plaintext secret is never written to any log, DB field, or struct that is persisted — only secret_hash is persisted
Document that expires values must be one of 0, 30, 60, 90 — validation is done upstream

subtasks.title: Implement key lifecycle DB operations (create, revoke, refresh)
details: Create internal/keys/store.go

Implement CreateKey(ctx context.Context, tx *sql.Tx, userID string, expiresInDays int) (*KeyRecord, string, error): generates key_id and secret, stores secret_hash, returns KeyRecord and plaintext secret
Implement RevokeActiveKey(ctx context.Context, tx *sql.Tx, userID string) error: sets revoked_at=now() on rows where user_id=userID AND revoked_at IS NULL; no-op if no active key

Implement RefreshKey(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, keyID string) (*KeyRecord, string, error): generates new secret, updates secret_hash and recalculates expires_at using original expiry duration (original_days = days between created_at and original expires_at); returns 404 if key not found or revoked_at IS NOT NULL
Implement RevokeKey(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, keyID string) error: sets revoked_at=now() on first call; no-op (idempotent) if already revoked; returns 404 if key_id not found
KeyRecord struct: KeyID, UserID, CreatedAt, ExpiresAt (*time.Time), RevokedAt (*time.Time)

checks: go test ./internal/keys/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestGenerate|TestHash|TestStore|TestCreate|TestRevoke|TestRefresh' — all TS-02-8, TS-02-9, TS-02-27, TS-02-31 through TS-02-36 tests pass
go vet ./internal/keys/... — no vet warnings
02-REQ-11.1: grep -r 'math/rand' internal/keys/ returns no results
02-REQ-11.2: no DB column other than secret_hash contains plaintext secret
02-REQ-11.3: all generated key_ids match ^[0-9A-Za-z]{8}$; all secrets match ^[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$
02-REQ-2.5: RevokeActiveKey is a no-op when no active key exists (returns nil, not error)
No panic('not implemented') stubs remain in internal/keys/

subtasks.title: Implement callback request validation (provider, expires, redirect_uri)
details: In internal/auth/handlers.go, implement validateCallbackRequest(body CallbackRequest, registry *Registry, allowlist *Allowlist) error
Step 1: check provider against registry; if not found, return structured HTTP 400 error
Step 2: if expires supplied, validate it is one of [0, 30, 60, 90]; if invalid, return HTTP 400 with accepted values listed
Step 3: validate redirect_uri origin against allowlist; if not allowed, return HTTP 400
Step 4 (config check): if allowlist is in a state that requires external_url but it's absent (production mode, no external_url), return HTTP 500
CallbackRequest struct: Provider string, Code string, State string (optional), RedirectURI string, Expires *int (pointer to distinguish omitted from 0)
Default expires to 90 when omitted (nil pointer)

subtasks.title: Implement OAuth code exchange and user info retrieval with timeout
details: In internal/auth/handlers.go, implement exchangeAndValidate(ctx context.Context, provider Provider, code, redirectURI string) (*UserInfo, error)
Create a child context with timeout (e.g. 10s) using context.WithTimeout; pass to provider.ExchangeCode and provider.GetUserInfo
If ExchangeCode returns error (network failure, non-2xx, parse error, timeout): return HTTP 502 error
If GetUserInfo returns error: return HTTP 502 error
After retrieval: check email is non-null and non-empty; if empty, return HTTP 400
Validate UserInfo.Login against username rules (^[0-9A-Za-z-]{1,39}$); if invalid, return HTTP 400
Return validated *UserInfo on success

subtasks.title: Implement user upsert, key lifecycle transaction in callback handler
details: Implement upsertUserInTransaction(ctx context.Context, tx *sql.Tx, info *UserInfo, provider string) (*User, error)
Case create: no user for (provider, provider_id) — insert new row with uuid.New().String() as id, status='active', store provider_id
Case update: existing user — UPDATE username and email from info; do NOT update provider_id; bump updated_at only if username or email value actually changed
Check username uniqueness case-insensitively against other (provider, provider_id) pairs; if conflict, return HTTP 409
After upsert: check user.Status; if 'blocked', return HTTP 403 — do not proceed to key generation
Begin DB transaction; call RevokeActiveKey, then CreateKey within same tx; commit; rollback on any error returning HTTP 500
Assemble and return HTTP 200 response: user object (id, username, email, full_name, status, provider, created_at, updated_at) + api_key object (key_id, secret, token, user_id, created_at, expires_at)

checks: go test ./internal/auth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestCallback' — all TS-02-5 through TS-02-9 and TS-02-E2 through TS-02-E12 tests pass
go vet ./internal/auth/... — no vet warnings
02-REQ-2.1 acceptance criteria met: valid callback returns HTTP 200 with user and api_key; all DB writes in single transaction
02-PROP-5 verified: inject failure at each tx step; DB state is unchanged (no partial writes)
02-PROP-8 verified: provider_id column unchanged after second login for same (provider, provider_id)
No panic('not implemented') stubs remain in callback handler

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/v1/users (admin create user)
details: Create internal/users/handlers.go
Implement CreateUserHandler(db *sql.DB, registry *Registry) echo.HandlerFunc protected by admin middleware from server_foundation
Parse body: username (required), email (required), full_name (optional), provider (required, non-empty), provider_id (required, non-empty)
Validate username against ^[0-9A-Za-z-]{1,39}$; return HTTP 400 if invalid
Validate provider_id is non-empty; return HTTP 400 otherwise
If provider not in registry: log warning (slog.Warn or log.Printf) but proceed with user creation
Generate UUID v4 via google/uuid.New().String(); set status='active'
Check case-insensitive username uniqueness and (provider, provider_id) uniqueness; return HTTP 409 on conflict
Insert into users table; return HTTP 201 with user object EXCLUDING provider_id; no api_keys row created

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/users and GET /api/v1/users/:id handlers
details: Implement ListUsersHandler(db *sql.DB) echo.HandlerFunc protected by admin middleware
Query users table ORDER BY created_at ASC; return {users: []} on empty result
Each entry: id, username, email, full_name, status, provider, created_at, updated_at — omit provider_id
Implement GetUserHandler(db *sql.DB) echo.HandlerFunc protected by admin middleware
Query users table by :id (UUID); return HTTP 404 with server_foundation structured error if not found
Include all user fields including provider_id; join with team_memberships table to populate team_memberships array [{team_id, team_name, role}]; return empty array if no memberships
Return HTTP 403 for non-admin callers on both endpoints

subtasks.title: Implement PUT /api/v1/users/:id handler with field-level auth
details: Implement UpdateUserHandler(db *sql.DB) echo.HandlerFunc; requires any authenticated caller (admin or user API key)
If caller is non-admin and target :id != caller's own id: return HTTP 403
If caller is non-admin and request body includes 'status' field: return HTTP 403 (even if other fields present)
Parse body: full_name (optional, null or empty string sets DB value to NULL), status (optional, admin only)
Load current user from DB; return HTTP 404 if not found
Compare incoming values to stored values; build UPDATE SET only for changed fields
If at least one field changes: execute UPDATE including updated_at = now(); if no changes: skip UPDATE (no-op)
Return HTTP 200 with updated user object including provider_id but excluding team_memberships
Admin self-update: admin token bypasses the non-admin restriction even for target == caller

checks: go test ./internal/users/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestCreate|TestList|TestGet|TestUpdate' — all TS-02-13 through TS-02-23, TS-02-37 through TS-02-39, TS-02-E14 through TS-02-E22, TS-02-E28, TS-02-E29 tests pass
go vet ./internal/users/... — no vet warnings
02-REQ-4.1 acceptance criteria met: provider_id excluded from POST /api/v1/users response; no api_keys row created
02-REQ-7.3 and 02-PROP-7: no-op PUT does not update updated_at in DB (verified by querying DB directly in tests)
02-PROP-4: no two users rows have same LOWER(username) (TS-02-P4 passes)
No panic('not implemented') stubs remain in internal/users/

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/keys list handler
details: Create internal/keys/handlers.go
Implement ListKeysHandler(db *sql.DB) echo.HandlerFunc
If caller is admin token: SELECT key_id, user_id, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at FROM api_keys ORDER BY created_at ASC
If caller is user API key: SELECT ... FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = caller.UserID ORDER BY created_at ASC (all historical keys including expired and revoked)
If caller is workspace token: return HTTP 403 with structured error body
Never include secret, secret_hash, token, status, is_active, or is_valid fields in response
Return {keys: [...]}; return {keys: []} on empty result

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh handler
details: Implement RefreshKeyHandler(db *sql.DB) echo.HandlerFunc
Lookup key_id from api_keys table; if not found OR revoked_at IS NOT NULL: return HTTP 404 (revoked keys treated as non-existent)
Authorization: if caller is non-admin and key.user_id != caller's user ID: return HTTP 403
Admin may refresh keys of blocked users — do NOT check user status in this handler
Ignore any 'expires' field in request body; always use original expiry duration from key record
Compute original expiry N: if key.expires_at is null, N=0 (indefinite); else N = ceil((expires_at - created_at).Hours() / 24)
Call GenerateSecret(); hash with HashSecret(); update secret_hash and expires_at (ComputeExpiresAt(N, time.Now())) in DB
Return HTTP 200 with {key_id, user_id, secret (plaintext), token, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at: null}

subtasks.title: Implement DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id revoke handler
details: Implement RevokeKeyHandler(db *sql.DB) echo.HandlerFunc
Lookup key_id in api_keys table; if no row found: return HTTP 404 with structured error body
Authorization: if caller is non-admin and key.user_id != caller's user ID: return HTTP 403
Admin may revoke keys of blocked users — do NOT check user status
If key already has revoked_at IS NOT NULL: return HTTP 204 (idempotent no-op; do not update revoked_at)
Otherwise: UPDATE api_keys SET revoked_at = now() WHERE key_id = :key_id; return HTTP 204 with no body

subtasks.title: Register all routes on the Echo router
details: Create internal/server/routes.go (or extend existing from server_foundation)
Register public route: GET /api/v1/auth/providers (no auth middleware)
Register callback route: POST /api/v1/auth/callback (no auth middleware — validates internally)
Register admin-only routes with server_foundation admin middleware: POST /api/v1/users, GET /api/v1/users, GET /api/v1/users/:id
Register mixed-auth routes with server_foundation any-auth middleware: PUT /api/v1/users/:id, GET /api/v1/keys, POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh, DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id
Wire handler constructors with actual db, registry, and allowlist dependencies — no stub constructors

task_groups.title: Wiring Verification — End-to-End Integration and Stub Audit

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths — verify handler chain completeness
details: Trace 02-PATH-1 (Full GitHub OAuth Login): verify GET /api/v1/auth/providers -> GetProvidersHandler -> Registry.Lookup -> ... -> POST /api/v1/auth/callback -> exchangeAndValidate -> upsertUserInTransaction -> RevokeActiveKey -> CreateKey -> HTTP 200 response with token; no step returns without calling next
Trace 02-PATH-2 (Admin Pre-provisions User): verify POST /api/v1/users -> CreateUserHandler -> DB insert -> HTTP 201; then callback finds existing user via (provider, provider_id) and creates key

Trace 02-PATH-3 (User Refreshes Expired Key): verify POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh -> RefreshKeyHandler -> DB lookup (non-revoked) -> GenerateSecret -> HashSecret -> ComputeExpiresAt -> DB update -> HTTP 200 with new token

Trace 02-PATH-4 (Admin Blocks User): verify PUT /api/v1/users/:id -> UpdateUserHandler -> DB update (status='blocked', updated_at bumped) -> DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id -> RevokeKeyHandler -> revoked_at set -> subsequent callback returns HTTP 403

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation across handler boundaries
details: Confirm CreateKey returns (*KeyRecord, plaintext_secret, error) and the caller in the callback handler uses all three values: KeyRecord for the response, plaintext_secret for building the token, error for rollback
Confirm RefreshKeyHandler receives new plaintext secret from GenerateSecret() and includes it in the HTTP 200 response body (not just storing the hash)
Confirm upsertUserInTransaction propagates (provider_id IS NOT updated on subsequent calls) — verified by reading provider_id back from DB and comparing
Confirm ComputeExpiresAt return value (*time.Time) is correctly stored as expires_at in the DB and returned in the API response (null for indefinite, ISO8601 for timed)

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with real components
details: Run: go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSmoke'
TS-02-SMOKE-1: GET /api/v1/auth/providers succeeds; POST /api/v1/auth/callback with mocked GitHub creates user + key; token format af_<8>_<32>; DB rows verified
TS-02-SMOKE-2: Admin POST /api/v1/users creates user without key; subsequent callback login creates key with 90-day expiry; one user row total
TS-02-SMOKE-3: POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh on expired (non-revoked) key returns new secret; expires_at recalculated using original duration
TS-02-SMOKE-4: PUT /api/v1/users/:id blocks user; DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id revokes key; subsequent callback returns HTTP 403; status not reactivated
All smoke tests use real Echo server, real in-memory SQLite DB, real key generation, mocked GitHub HTTP endpoints only

subtasks.title: Stub and dead-code audit
details: Run: grep -rn 'panic("not implemented")' internal/ — expect zero results
Run: grep -rn 'math/rand' internal/ — expect zero results (crypto/rand must be the only rand import)
Run: grep -rn '// TODO' internal/ — review any TODOs; none should block correctness
Run: grep -rn 'return nil' internal/keys/ internal/auth/ — review each bare return nil to confirm it is intentional (e.g. error-free no-op) and not an unfinished stub
Confirm all Provider interface methods are fully implemented in GitHubProvider (no method returning zero-value without logic)

subtasks.title: Cross-spec entry point verification with server_foundation
details: Verify server_foundation auth middleware is wired into Echo routes for all protected endpoints: admin middleware on POST /api/v1/users, GET /api/v1/users, GET /api/v1/users/:id; any-auth middleware on PUT /api/v1/users/:id, GET /api/v1/keys, POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh, DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id
Confirm workspace token detection (from server_foundation middleware) correctly returns HTTP 403 on GET /api/v1/keys (TS-02-E23)
Confirm server_foundation's structured error helper functions are called — not raw c.JSON with ad-hoc maps — for all 404 and 400 error responses in this spec
Confirm server_foundation's DB schema (users, api_keys tables with all columns referenced in this spec) is present and matches the SQL queries in internal/users/ and internal/keys/
